skill.name: code-review-assistant
skill.description: Reviews pull request code for security, performance, quality, and best-practice issues, then summarizes actionable feedback.
skill.summary: AI-assisted pull request review for safer and cleaner code changes.
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a weak cryptography pattern at SKILL.md line 4 and high entropy at SKILL.md line 1. Manual review found only Markdown front matter and a Chinese natural-language description, with no scripts, commands, network calls, secret access, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged line is a natural-language skill description about code review, security scanning, performance analysis, best practices, and review reports. No cryptographic API, algorithm selection, or executable code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is Markdown front matter containing a Chinese description. There is no code path or cryptographic operation to misuse.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High File Entropy
low_findings.description: The flagged file begins with standard YAML front matter and contains short Markdown documentation. The non-ASCII text likely increased entropy scoring, but no encoded payload, binary data, or encrypted content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found readable Markdown only. The file has no obfuscated blocks, long encoded strings, or hidden executable content.

content.value_statement: Manual pull request review can miss security, performance, and maintainability issues. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to produce structured review feedback with clear priorities.
actual_capabilities: Reviews pull request changes for security, performance, and maintainability concerns.
Highlights best-practice gaps that may affect long-term code quality.
Produces review summaries suitable for team discussion.
Supports developer workflows in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
Reminds users to manually verify important AI-generated findings.
limitations: Does not execute tests or scan repositories by itself.
Depends on the code context and prompts provided by the user.
May miss issues that require runtime data or production context.
Requires human review before accepting critical recommendations.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Check a proposed change for security, performance, and maintainability risks before merge.

use_cases.title: Freelance Delivery Check
use_cases.description: Create a concise quality report before sending code changes to a client.

use_cases.title: Team Review Support
use_cases.description: Give reviewers a structured first pass so they can focus on domain-specific decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Review
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner wants clear feedback on a small change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request for obvious bugs, security issues, performance concerns, and maintainability problems. Group findings by priority.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer wants extra attention on risky behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change with a security focus. Look for injection risks, unsafe data handling, secret exposure, access control gaps, and risky dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Performance and Quality Review
prompt_templates.scenario: A team wants feedback on efficiency and code health.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code for performance, complexity, resource use, test coverage, and best-practice gaps. Include practical improvement suggestions.

prompt_templates.title: Release Gate Review
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user needs a decision-ready review summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as a senior reviewer for this release candidate. Provide blocking issues, non-blocking concerns, test recommendations, and a final merge recommendation.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that changes authentication middleware.
output_examples.output: The review identifies a blocking authorization bypass risk, asks for tests around expired sessions, and suggests clearer error handling.

output_examples.input: Check a database query optimization change.
output_examples.output: The review notes improved query scope, flags a missing index migration, and recommends measuring results with representative data.

output_examples.input: Summarize quality concerns before sending client work.
output_examples.output: The review produces a short report with high-priority fixes, minor cleanup items, and manual verification steps.

best_practices: Provide the diff, related files, and expected behavior for the change.
Ask for findings grouped by severity and include a merge recommendation.
Verify important findings with tests, logs, or a second reviewer.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for approval based only on a short summary of the change.
Do not treat AI review output as a replacement for human ownership.
Do not ignore project-specific standards, tests, or release requirements.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews provided code changes for security, performance, maintainability, best practices, and review-report quality.

faq.question: Does it run automated security scanners?
faq.answer: No. It guides AI review based on the context you provide. Use dedicated scanners when required.

faq.question: Can it replace a human reviewer?
faq.answer: No. It is an assistant for first-pass review and structured feedback. Human review is still required.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The skill states support for Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatibility.

faq.question: What input should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the pull request diff, relevant files, expected behavior, known risks, and any project review standards.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual audit found only Markdown instructions and no confirmed dangerous behavior.
